title: Healthcare Delivery Systems
description: Analysis and design of healthcare delivery systems using industrial engineering methods. Topics include patient flow, capacity planning, scheduling, quality improvement, and simulation in healthcare settings.

title: Quality Control
description: Statistical methods for quality control and improvement. Topics include control charts, acceptance sampling, process capability, design of experiments, and Six Sigma methodology.

title: Reliability
description: Fundamentals of reliability engineering. Topics include failure distributions, reliability modeling, system reliability, maintainability, and life testing methods.

title: Industrial Applications of Statistics
description: Applied statistical methods for industrial engineering problems. Topics include regression analysis, design of experiments, ANOVA, and response surface methodology.

title: Linear Programming
description: Theory and methods of linear programming. Topics include simplex method, duality theory, sensitivity analysis, and applications in operations research.

title: Stochastic Models in Operations Research I
description: Introduction to stochastic models including Markov chains, queuing theory, and stochastic processes with applications in operations research and engineering.

title: Engineering Economic Analysis
description: Economic analysis methods for engineering decisions. Topics include time value of money, cost estimation, depreciation, replacement analysis, and benefit-cost analysis.

title: Economic Decisions in Engineering
description: Advanced topics in engineering economy including decision analysis under uncertainty, risk analysis, real options, and multi-criteria decision making.

title: Job Design
description: Principles of job and work design. Topics include work measurement, time study, methods engineering, workplace layout, and ergonomic considerations in job design.

title: Safety Engineering
description: Principles and methods of safety engineering. Topics include hazard identification, risk assessment, safety management systems, accident investigation, and regulatory compliance.

title: Cognitive Engineering of Interactive Software
description: Design and evaluation of interactive software systems from a cognitive engineering perspective. Topics include mental models, information processing, interface design principles, usability testing, and user-centered design methods.

title: Introduction to Convex Optimization
description: Convex optimization theory and algorithms. Topics include convex sets and functions, optimization problems, duality, interior-point methods, and applications in engineering.

title: Production Management Control
description: Methods for production planning, scheduling, and inventory control. Topics include MRP, capacity planning, shop floor control, and just-in-time systems.

title: Manufacturing Process Engineering
description: Engineering analysis of manufacturing processes. Topics include process selection, process planning, machining, forming, joining, and advanced manufacturing technologies.

title: Human Factors in Engineering
description: Principles of human factors engineering. Topics include human capabilities and limitations, human-machine interaction, display and control design, workplace design, usability evaluation, and human error analysis.

title: Applied Ergonomics
description: Applied methods in ergonomics and human factors. Topics include physical ergonomics, biomechanics, anthropometry, work physiology, and ergonomic assessment tools.

title: Design and Control of Production and Manufacturing Systems
description: Design, analysis, and control of production systems. Topics include facility layout, material flow, production scheduling, and system performance analysis.

title: Systems Simulation
description: Discrete-event simulation modeling and analysis. Topics include simulation methodology, random number generation, input modeling, output analysis, and simulation software.

title: Advanced Facilities Design
description: Advanced topics in facilities planning and design. Topics include layout optimization, material handling system design, warehouse design, and computer-aided facilities planning.

title: e-Work and e-Service
description: Design and management of electronic work and service systems. Topics include distributed work, collaboration technologies, service engineering, and digital transformation.

title: Project Management
description: Principles and methods of project management. Topics include project planning, scheduling, resource allocation, risk management, earned value management, and agile methodologies.

title: Risk Analysis Methods and Applications
description: Methods for risk analysis and decision making under uncertainty. Topics include probabilistic risk assessment, decision trees, Monte Carlo simulation, and risk communication.

title: Supply Chain Engineering & Analytics
description: Engineering and analytical approaches to supply chain design and management. Topics include network design, inventory optimization, demand forecasting, and supply chain analytics.

title: Quality Systems Management
description: Management of quality systems including ISO standards, total quality management, quality auditing, and continuous improvement frameworks.

title: Machine Learning and its Applications
description: Introduction to machine learning with applications in industrial engineering. Topics include supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, and practical applications.

title: Machine Learning
description: Fundamentals of machine learning including supervised learning, unsupervised learning, deep learning, and reinforcement learning with engineering applications.

title: Computer Network Systems
description: Principles of computer networking. Topics include network architecture, protocols, routing, security, and performance analysis.

title: Introduction to Data Management
description: Fundamentals of data management including database design, SQL, data modeling, data warehousing, and data processing systems.

title: Introduction to Robotic Systems
description: Fundamentals of robotic systems including kinematics, dynamics, control, sensors, and autonomous systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Fundamentals of artificial intelligence including search, knowledge representation, reasoning, planning, and machine learning.

title: Introduction to Data Mining
description: Principles of data mining including classification, clustering, association rules, anomaly detection, and applications in large-scale data analysis.

title: Engineering Design: A Decision-Based Perspective
description: Engineering design theory and methodology using decision-based framework. Topics include design under uncertainty, multi-objective optimization, utility theory, and decision analysis in engineering design.

title: Product and Process Design
description: Methodologies for product and process design including concept generation, design for manufacturing, prototyping, and design optimization.

title: Design for Manufacturability
description: Principles and methods for designing products for efficient manufacturing. Topics include DFM guidelines, cost estimation, process selection, and design-process interaction.

title: Reliability-Based Design
description: Probabilistic methods for engineering design considering reliability. Topics include failure mode analysis, reliability prediction, and robust design.

title: Theory and Design of Control Systems
description: Theory and design of feedback control systems. Topics include system modeling, stability analysis, controller design, and digital control.

title: Perspectives on Systems
description: Interdisciplinary perspectives on systems thinking. Explores complex systems concepts, emergence, feedback loops, and systemic approaches to engineering challenges.

title: Tools and Methodologies for Designing Systems
description: Practical tools and methodologies for systems design including model-based systems engineering, requirements engineering, and systems architecture.

title: Practical Systems Thinking
description: Applied systems thinking methods for complex problem solving. Topics include causal loop diagrams, system dynamics, stakeholder analysis, and systemic intervention design.

title: System-of-Systems Modeling and Analysis
description: Modeling and analysis of system-of-systems including architecture frameworks, interoperability, emergence, and large-scale system integration.

title: Systems Analysis and Synthesis
description: Systems engineering analysis and synthesis methods. Topics include requirements analysis, functional analysis, trade studies, and system verification and validation.

title: Technical Project Management
description: Project management methods for engineering projects. Topics include project planning, scheduling, budgeting, risk management, and team leadership in technical contexts.

title: Quality Systems for Regulatory Compliance
description: Quality management systems for regulated industries. Topics include FDA regulations, ISO 13485, design controls, CAPA, and quality system auditing.

title: Lean Manufacturing of Materials
description: Lean manufacturing principles applied to materials processing. Topics include value stream mapping, waste elimination, continuous flow, pull systems, and kaizen.

title: Smart Manufacturing in Process Industries
description: Smart manufacturing concepts for process industries. Topics include IoT, digital twins, advanced process control, data analytics, and Industry 4.0.

title: Construction Project Control Systems
description: Methods for planning and controlling construction projects. Topics include scheduling, cost control, resource management, and project performance measurement.

title: Entrepreneurship and Business Strategy in Engineering
description: Core skills required to build and grow engineering- and technology-based businesses through lecture, case discussions, and weekly activities tied to a semester-long team project. Content includes market analysis techniques, combinatorial business design and planning methods, strategic innovation theories, competitive analysis, methods of emergent strategy and risk mitigation, team building, and organizational design principles.

title: Engineering Leadership
description: Leadership principles and practices for engineers. Topics include leadership styles, team dynamics, communication, conflict resolution, and leading technical organizations.

title: Social Impact Startup Academy
description: Entrepreneurship program focused on building startups that address social challenges. Topics include lean startup methodology, social enterprise models, impact measurement, and venture development.

title: Social Innovation and Business
description: Explores the intersection of social innovation and business strategy. Topics include design thinking for social impact, business model innovation, and scaling social ventures.

title: Corporate Consulting (ELI)
description: Experiential learning through real corporate consulting engagements. Students work on actual business problems for partner companies, applying strategic analysis, data-driven recommendations, and professional presentation skills.

title: Student Managed Venture Fund
description: Students manage a real venture capital fund, evaluating startup investments. Topics include due diligence, valuation, deal structuring, portfolio management, and venture capital strategy.

title: Strategic Management
description: Frameworks for strategic analysis and decision making. Topics include competitive strategy, industry analysis, resource-based view, corporate strategy, and strategic implementation.

title: Introduction to Operations Management
description: Fundamentals of operations management. Topics include process analysis, capacity management, inventory management, supply chain management, quality, and lean operations.

title: Management of Operating Systems
description: Advanced operations management covering system design and operation. Topics include scheduling, project management, supply chain coordination, and technology management.

title: Business Analytics
description: Data-driven decision making for business. Topics include descriptive analytics, predictive modeling, prescriptive analytics, and data visualization for managerial decisions.

title: New Product Development
description: Management of new product development processes. Topics include idea generation, concept testing, product design, market testing, and launch strategies.

title: Project Management
description: Project management from a business perspective. Topics include project selection, planning, scheduling, resource management, risk assessment, and project governance.

title: Strategic Sourcing and Procurement
description: Strategic approaches to sourcing and procurement management. Topics include supplier selection, negotiation, contract management, and procurement strategy.

title: Global Supply Chain Management
description: Management of global supply chains. Topics include supply chain strategy, logistics, distribution, global sourcing, risk management, and sustainability in supply chains.

title: Data Mining
description: Techniques for extracting knowledge from large datasets. Topics include classification, clustering, association rules, text mining, and practical applications in business.

title: Python Programming
description: Python programming for business applications. Topics include data manipulation, automation, web scraping, data visualization, and building analytical tools.

title: Technology Driven Business
description: Management of technology-driven businesses. Topics include technology strategy, digital transformation, platform economics, and managing innovation in tech organizations.

title: Developing a Global Business Strategy
description: Development of global business strategies. Topics include international market entry, cross-cultural management, global operations, and multinational strategy.

title: Storytelling with Data
description: Effective data communication and visualization for business audiences. Topics include narrative structure, visual design principles, dashboard creation, and persuasive data presentation.

title: Launching Global Leaders
description: Leadership development program for global business contexts. Topics include cross-cultural leadership, global team management, and international business communication.

title: AI, Ethics, and Society
description: Ethical, social, and policy implications of artificial intelligence. Topics include bias, fairness, privacy, accountability, and governance of AI systems.

title: Communication for Engineering Leaders
description: Advanced communication skills for engineering leaders. Topics include executive communication, technical writing, stakeholder management, and persuasive presentations.

title: AI for Business Decisions
description: How AI can be utilized to create value and sustainable competitive advantage. Teaches the scientific process of using AI to transform data into predictions and insights for making better business decisions in a real-world setting.

title: Introduction to Scientific Machine Learning
description: Machine learning methods for scientific and engineering applications. Topics include neural networks, physics-informed ML, surrogate modeling, and data-driven discovery.

title: Optimization Methods for Systems and Control
description: Optimization methods for systems and control problems. Topics include convex optimization, linear and nonlinear programming, and optimal control applications.

title: Design for IP Protection and Commercialization
description: Intellectual property strategy for engineering products. Topics include patents, trade secrets, IP portfolio management, and technology commercialization.

title: Discrete Optimization Models and Algorithms
description: Integer programming, combinatorial optimization, and network optimization. Topics include branch and bound, cutting planes, heuristics, and graph algorithms.

title: Design and Evaluation of Material Handling Systems
description: Design principles for material handling systems. Topics include conveyor systems, automated guided vehicles, warehouse systems, and system simulation.

title: Leadership and Advanced Project Management
description: Advanced project management and leadership skills for construction and engineering management contexts. Topics include earned value, risk management, and leadership development.

title: Innovation Science and Socio-Technical Change
description: Explores innovation science frameworks and methods for driving socio-technical change. Students learn to identify opportunities, frame complex problems, and design holistic solutions that account for technological, economic, social, and cultural dimensions of innovation adoption and impact.

title: The Data Mine Corporate Partners
description: Experiential learning program where students work on real-world data science projects with corporate partners. Students apply data analytics, machine learning, and problem-solving skills to address actual business challenges.
